author: śrīla narottama⋅dāsa ṭhākura
verses:
‘gaurāṅga’ balite habe pulaka śarīra
‘hari hari’ balite nayane ba’be nīra

āra kabe nitāicā̐dera karuṇā hoibe
saṁsāra⋅vāsanā mora kabe tuccha ha’be

viṣaya chāḍiyā kabe śuddha ha’be mana
kabe hāma heraba śrī-vṛndāvana

rūpa⋅raghunātha⋅pade hoibe ākuti
kabe hāma bujhaba se jugala⋅pīriti

rūpa⋅raghunātha⋅pade rahu mora āśa
prārthanā karaye sadā narottama⋅dāsa
en_translation:
When will my body shiver while saying 'Gaurāṅga'? When will tears flow from my eyes while saying 'Hari Hari'?

When will Nitāi-cānda be merciful? When will my worldly desires become insignificant?

When will my mind be purified by giving up sense objects? When will I see Śrī Vṛndāvana?

When will there be eagerness for the feet of Rūpa and Raghunātha? When will I understand the love of the Divine Couple?

My hope rests at the feet of Rūpa and Raghunātha; Narottama Dāsa always makes this prayer.
